title: Luscious Shattering French Apples Tart
description: A buttery laminated tart with caramelized apples and a sweet applesauce base, served warm on a Talavera plate.
instructions: Roll out the puff pastry sheet on a lightly floured surface to smooth any folds, then cut into 6 equal rectangles.
In a bowl, toss the sliced apples with sugar, cinnamon, butter, and a pinch of salt until coated.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and sauté the apples for about 5 minutes until they begin to soften.
Spread a thin layer of applesauce over the bottom of each puff pastry rectangle.
Top the applesauce with the sautéed apples, arranging them in overlapping rows to create a shattering effect.
Fold each rectangle into a loose pocket, trimming any excess pastry and pinching the edges to seal.
Brush the tops of the pockets with beaten egg to give them a golden glaze.
Place the tart pockets on a baking sheet lined with parchment, then bake for 20 minutes or until puffed and golden.
Let the tarts cool for 5 minutes before transferring them to a Talavera plate for serving.
